C1_evol: damage is compatible w/ declining selection but doesn't derive it
C2_kinetics: random damage gives ~linear hazard; needs add-ons for exact Gompertz
C3_crossspecies: predicts metabolic-rate scaling, but birds/bats/NMR break it
C4_hallmarks: explains genomic-instability hallmark; not epigenetic reset
C5_interventions: predicts antioxidants extend LS — ITP: 0/8 do (p=4e-4)
C6_reset: irreversible damage cannot be reset by reprogramming (Lu 2020)
fixed mutations not reset by reprogramming

C1_evol: noise compatible but not derived from selection
C3_crossspecies: clock rate varies; dial not derived
C4_hallmarks: epigenetic alteration central; links to others claimed
C5_interventions: reprogramming works; unclear why mTOR-lowering works, why antiox fail
C6_reset: predicts reprogramming resets age (Lu2020,Yang2023) — its strength
C7_placement: frames driver as entropy/noise = damage-like pole

C1_evol: quasi-program: programs run on because selection can't stop them (Williams/Hamilton)
C2_kinetics: hyperfunction pathology accelerates; exact Gompertz not derived
C3_crossspecies: growth-rate/TOR differences plausible; dial not formalized
C4_hallmarks: many hallmarks as downstream of continued signaling
C5_interventions: predicts mTOR/growth-lowering works, antioxidants need not — matches ITP
C6_reset: reprogramming compatible but not central to theory

C1_evol: derives soma/germline asymmetry from resource allocation (Kirkwood)
C2_kinetics: predicts maintenance underinvestment; shape not forced
C4_hallmarks: hallmarks = under-maintained damage; co-occurrence not forced
C5_interventions: DR reallocates to maintenance; why antioxidants fail unclear
C6_reset: germline low-damage; reprogramming reset not central

C1_evol: no selected post-reproductive target => open-loop is the DIRECT consequence of Hamilton
C2_kinetics: generative model assumes the exponential hazard form, so it shows sufficiency for Gompertz+plateau, not a derivation
C3_crossspecies: the loop-closure account of the cross-species dial is consistent but not independently derived; NMR flatness fits several theories
C4_hallmarks: hallmarks = correlated readouts of ONE drifting control state (co-occurrence forced)
C5_interventions: lowering signaling re-imposes youth set-point (works); antioxidants touch byproducts not the control error (fail) — matches ITP p=4e-4
C6_reset: reprogramming rewrites regulatory state to young target; germline re-runs development => lineage young
C7_placement: quasi-programmed by derivation: deterministic trajectory, forced by clock regularity+antiox failure+reset

coding_note: Winner coded by evidential tier: 'predicts' (P) is used only where a prediction is earned independent of the model's assumptions — C1 (direct evolutionary derivation) and C5 (the held-out ITP test, p=4e-4). C2 and C3 are 'explains' (E): the generative model assumes the Gompertz hazard form rather than deriving it, and the cross-species account is consistent but not uniquely so. C4, C6, C7 are 'explains' because their evidence is shared with rival theories and does not uniquely discriminate. Scores: P=+2, E=+1, C=0, X=-2, minus an added-assumption penalty.
